message.content: Adversaries may target user email on local systems to collect sensitive information, exploiting files such as Outlook storage or cache files (.ost and .pst). These files can be substantial, with .ost files supporting up to 50GB in Outlook 2010 and later versions. Common adversary tools and methods include GETMAIL and MAPIGET for extracting emails, recursive searches for .pst files, and modules that scrape email data. Notable groups like APT1, Carbanak, Chimera, CosmicDuke, and Emotet have been observed using these techniques. The impact of such email collection can be significant, leading to data breaches, espionage, and further phishing attacks. Mitigation strategies include implementing strong access controls, encrypting email storage files, regularly updating and patching email clients, and monitoring for unusual file access patterns. Additionally, user education on recognizing phishing attempts and securing email accounts can help reduce the risk of exploitation.
